arts.ttl: Garnacho 'agreement in principle' amid Chelsea transfer twist and United talks
arts.sum: football.london says: Man United reputedly rejected a bid around that range last week for Garnacho, but the reports of him agreeing to a deal could force Chelsea to decide whether they want the winger. Garnacho is being spoken about behind the scenes as the club looks at signing a left winger, with Jamie Gittens and Mathys Tel also receiving mentions in west London this month.

arts.ttl: Vitor Pereira rages at Matheus Cunha for his behaviour during Chelsea defeat and hints Wolves star's head has been turned by transfer rumours
arts.sum: KIERAN GILL AT STAMFORD BRIDGE: Vitor Pereira did not hold back in his criticism of Matheus Cunha, who performed poorly in Wolves' 3-1 defeat by Chelsea on Monday night.

arts.ttl: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal eye move for Wolves star Cunha
arts.sum: Arsenal have ramped up their interest in Wolverhampton Wanderers forward Matheus Cunha, while Barcelona have added Bayern Munich's Jamal Musiala to their shortlist amid plans to make a marquee signing in the summer. The Gunners are believed to be interested in a potential move for the 25-year-old as manager Mikel Arteta looks to reinforce his forward line, however it is reported that it would take a "crazy" offer in excess of £80 million to persuade Vitor Pereira's side to let the Brazil international go in the middle of the season as they look to avoid relegation from the Premier League .

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca comes out fighting for under-fire Robert Sanchez after the Chelsea keeper's latest howler
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca defended Robert Sanchez after the Chelsea goalkeeper's latest mistake on Monday night, insisting the £25million man saves them more points than he costs.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca leaps to defence of struggling Chelsea star - 'No problem'
arts.sum: Chelsea got their first win in six Premier League matches with a 3-1 victory over Wolves on Monday night, but goalkeeper Robert Sanchez is again under the microscope after another error

arts.ttl: Maresca welcomes Chelsea win – but wants more from Madueke
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca was pleased Chelsea ended their winless run – but wants more from Noni Madueke. The Blues beat Wolves 3-1 at Stamford Bridge, where second-half goals from Madueke and Marc Cucurella secured a first victory in six league matches. After Tosin Adarabioyo’s third goal in as many games put the hosts ahead, Matt Doherty […] The post Maresca welcomes Chelsea win – but wants more from Madueke appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Chelsea agree £11.9m deal for Strasbourg's Sarr
arts.sum: Chelsea are set to sign French defender Mamadou Sarr from partner club Strasbourg.

arts.ttl: Chelsea’s Noni Madueke ordered to improve work rate by boss Enzo Maresca
arts.sum: Maresca said Madueke ‘stopped playing a little bit’ after scoring in the win over Wolves.

arts.ttl: Wolves need to be consistent for 90 minutes - Pereira
arts.sum: Wolverhampton Wanderers manager Vitor Pereira says his players "need to be consistent for 90 minutes" after their 3-1 loss at Chelsea.

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer: Saint Kitts and proud
arts.sum: Chelsea and England forward Cole Palmer proudly owns his Saint Kitts and Nevis roots. DJ on the island of Saint Kitts, Leslie 'Sugar Bowl' Morton IV, tells BBC Sport what he means to the country.
